channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news curated from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇶💰 US blocks Iraq dollar shipments to pressure Tehran
item.description: The United States has halted dollar shipments to Iraq, forcing Baghdad to distance itself from Iran. The money comes from Iraq own oil revenues but the US controls its delivery.

item.title: 🇮🇳🗳️ Millions of voters purged from rolls before West Bengal election
item.description: India has removed millions from electoral rolls ahead of critical state elections. Critics say Muslims and minorities have been disproportionately affected by the voter list cleanup.

item.title: 💰⚔️ Condom prices could surge 30% due to Iran war
item.description: The world largest condom producer Karex warns prices may rise sharply as rubber supplies are disrupted by conflict in the Middle East. The Malaysia-based company supplies global markets.

item.title: ⚖️🌱 Federal judge strikes down Trump clean energy slowdown
item.description: A federal judge has blocked Trump administration actions that slowed clean energy projects, ruling the moves exceeded executive authority. The decision affects wind and solar developments.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇸 Anti-Trump sentiment unites European politics
item.description: Trump brand has sunk so low that conservatives are joining leftist politicians in opposing him, creating unexpected political unity across Europe. The dynamic is reshaping electoral coalitions.

item.title: ✈️⛽ Lufthansa cuts 20,000 summer flights as Iran war doubles jet fuel prices
item.description: German airline Lufthansa will cancel 20,000 European short-haul flights this summer, citing soaring fuel costs from the Strait of Hormuz closure. The carrier will stop flying to 10 cities and retire 27 aircraft as jet fuel prices have doubled since the US-Iran conflict began.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇰🇷 US restricts intelligence sharing with South Korea after minister reveals North Korean nuclear site
item.description: Washington has partially restricted satellite intelligence sharing with Seoul after South Korea's unification minister publicly identified a suspected North Korean uranium enrichment facility in Kusong. The move highlights growing tensions in the US-South Korea alliance under President Lee Jae Myung's conciliatory approach to Pyongyang.

item.title: ⛪🇬🇶 Pope Leo condemns prison conditions in Equatorial Guinea, calls for freedom
item.description: During his Africa tour finale, Pope Leo criticized Equatorial Guinea's prison conditions and urged the oil-rich nation to use its resources for all citizens, not just the elite. He addressed 100,000 people including President Obiang, the world's longest-serving leader, ahead of a visit to an infamous prison.

item.title: ⚖️🇸🇻 El Salvador begins mass trial of 486 alleged MS-13 gang members
item.description: A Salvadoran court started one of the biggest mass trials under President Bukele's emergency powers, prosecuting 486 alleged MS-13 members for over 47,000 crimes. Human rights groups warn the collective prosecution violates due process and blocks access to legal counsel.

item.title: 🇨🇴🇦🇷 Suspect in killing of Colombian presidential hopeful arrested in Argentina
item.description: Argentine authorities arrested a Colombian suspect allegedly involved in organizing the killing of Miguel Uribe, a prominent presidential candidate. The assassination shocked Colombia and raised questions about political violence ahead of elections.

item.title: 🇫🇷⚔️ Second French peacekeeper dies after ambush blamed on Hezbollah
item.description: A second French soldier has died from injuries sustained in a deadly ambush in southern Lebanon that French officials have blamed on Hezbollah. The attack marks an escalation in violence against UN peacekeeping forces in the region.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US slams China after African countries block Taiwan leader's Eswatini trip
item.description: The US condemned Beijing after several African countries revoked flight permits for Taiwan President Lai Ching-te's trip to Eswatini, one of Taiwan's few diplomatic allies. Washington accused China of pressuring nations to close airspace to Taiwanese leaders.

item.title: ⚽🌍 Trump envoy seeks to replace Iran with Italy at World Cup, report says
item.description: A Trump administration envoy has reportedly asked FIFA to replace Iran with Italy at the upcoming World Cup finals, citing the ongoing conflict. The request marks an unusual intersection of sports diplomacy and geopolitical pressure.

item.title: 🇮🇷💥 Iran ramps up attacks on ships in Strait of Hormuz after Trump ceasefire extension
item.description: Iran has intensified attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz despite the extended ceasefire announced by President Trump. The War Zone reports Iranian forces have targeted multiple ships, maintaining pressure on the critical waterway.

item.title: 🇺🇸🚢 USS Spruance blasts ship with deck gun in first such action in nearly four decades
item.description: The USS Spruance destroyer used its 5-inch deck gun against a vessel in a milestone engagement not seen since the 1980s. The incident highlights the evolving naval tactics in the Strait of Hormuz blockade operations.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚔️ Iran fires on three ships in Strait of Hormuz as US maintains blockade
item.description: Iranian forces opened fire on three commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz as the US continued its naval blockade amid stalled diplomatic talks. The escalation threatens to further disrupt global oil shipments through the critical chokepoint.

item.title: ⛪🇱🇧 Lebanon accuses Israel of blocking rescue after airstrike kills journalist
item.description: Lebanon's government says Israeli forces prevented rescuers from reaching the site of an airstrike that killed journalist Amal Khalil, accusing Israel of war crimes. The attack comes amid ongoing tensions along the Israel-Lebanon border.

item.title: 🏛️🇺🇸 US Navy Secretary leaving post immediately, Pentagon announces
item.description: US Navy Secretary John Phelan is departing his post effective immediately, the Pentagon said without providing explanation. The departure marks another high-ranking military leadership change as the US maintains a naval blockade of Iran.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Israeli strike kills journalist after ongoing attacks blocked rescuers, Lebanon says
item.description: Lebanon accused Israel of war crimes after an airstrike killed journalist Amal Khalil and ongoing attacks prevented Red Cross rescuers from reaching the site. The attack occurred in at-Tiri in southern Lebanon.

item.title: 🇮🇳🗳️ High-stakes West Bengal election begins amid voter roll controversy
item.description: India's West Bengal state began voting in critical elections amid controversy over a sweeping revision of electoral rolls. The polls are seen as a key test for opposition parties challenging the ruling BJP.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇦 EU approves 90 billion euro loan for Ukraine as pipeline deal breaks deadlock
item.description: The European Union has approved a 90 billion euro loan package for Ukraine after Hungary dropped its opposition. Ukraine agreed to reopen the Druzhba pipeline, ending months of stalemate over stalled oil supplies to Hungary.

item.title: 🇨🇳🌊 How China is building its next outpost at sea
item.description: China has been quietly and quickly building an island in disputed waters off the coast of Vietnam over the last few months. The New York Times visual investigation reveals the scale of the new outpost in the South China Sea.

item.title: 🇳🇬🇳 Nigeria's suspected coup plotters deny treason charges
item.description: Allegations of a coup plot first surfaced last year when an Independence Day parade was cancelled. The suspects deny treason charges as Nigeria grapples with political tensions.

item.title: 🇹🇼🌍 Taiwan president cancels trip after African countries revoke flight permits
item.description: Taiwan has accused Beijing of putting pressure on African countries to close their airspace to President Lai Ching-te's plane, forcing the cancellation of his trip to Eswatini.

item.title: 🇮🇷🕊️ To Iran, Trump blinked first by extending the ceasefire
item.description: Iran's leaders believe they can withstand an enduring standoff longer than President Trump. The strategy could be economically devastating for average Iranians, but Tehran sees the ceasefire extension as a US concession.
